purpose: Defines the !learn_update slash command — incrementally refreshes the Project Knowledge Index (PKI) at {project_root}/.synapse/knowledge/ by detecting and re-annotating only stale/new/deleted files. Five-phase workflow: (1) Load Existing PKI — read manifest.json, identify pre-flagged stale entries (PostToolUse hook set stale:true), find deleted files (in manifest but not on disk), find new files (on disk but not in manifest); (2) Detect Changes — compute SHA-256 hashes for non-stale files, compare against stored content_hash, add hash-mismatched files to stale set; (3) Re-scan via !p dispatch — group stale+new files by directory, dispatch parallel agents with previous annotations included for stale files so they produce focused diffs; (4) Merge — update manifest.json (content_hash, stale:false, last_annotated for re-annotated; new entries for new files; remove deleted entries; recompute stats), write annotation files to annotations/{hash}.json (delete old hash files for stale), rebuild domains.json and patterns.json from ALL annotations, clear queries/ cache; (5) Report. Auto-trigger at swarm start if stats.stale > 0.
gotchas: Only re-scan changed files — never re-read or re-annotate files whose content_hash matches AND stale:false. Speed is the entire point of this command.
Preserve unchanged annotations VERBATIM — do not rewrite/rephrase/improve them; only touch stale, new, and deleted entries.

PostToolUse hook sets stale:true when agents write to files during a swarm; that is the PRIMARY staleness signal. Content hash comparison is the SECONDARY fallback for changes that bypass the hook (manual edits, non-Synapse processes).
Handle deleted files cleanly: remove manifest entry AND delete annotation file from annotations/ — never leave orphaned annotation files.
ALWAYS rebuild domains.json and patterns.json after merging, even for one-file changes — index consistency > speed here.
ALWAYS clear queries/ cache after any update — stale cached bundles are worse than no cache.
Include previous annotations in agent prompts for stale files — agents should produce focused diffs, not regenerate from scratch.
If diff is large (50+ files), warn user and suggest !learn for full rebuild — incremental works best for small-to-medium changes.
If zero changes detected, exit immediately with 'PKI is up to date' — do NOT dispatch agents or rewrite files.
Update manifest atomically: read full file → apply all changes in memory → write complete file. Never partial writes or incremental appends.
Auto-trigger at swarm start: silent unless stale count >50 (suggest !learn) or manifest missing (suggest !learn).
Annotation file hash is shasum of relative PATH (stable); content_hash is shasum of CONTENT (changes with file).
patterns: Five-phase pipeline: load → detect → re-scan → merge → report.
Two-tier staleness detection: hook flag (primary) + content hash diff (secondary).
Parallel dispatch only for changed files — unchanged files never re-read.
Atomic file updates — read-modify-write, never append.
Auto-trigger integration: swarm masters call !learn_update silently before planning when stats.stale > 0.
conventions: Index rebuild after every merge regardless of change size.
Query cache wiped on any update.
